title: China's soybean imports pivot south: U.S. shipments vanish in September as South America fills the gap
section_path: China's soybean imports pivot south: U.S. shipments vanish in September as South America fills the gap
section_path_text: China's soybean imports pivot south: U.S. shipments vanish in September as South America fills the gap
summary:
Main
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2025-10-24_chinas-soybean-imports-pivot-south-us-shipments-vanish-in-september-as-south-ame_img_unnamed_54a89bf5d3e3.png
China's soybean supply chain took a sharp turn south in September. No U.S.- origin soybeans were discharged at Chinese ports, the first zero month since 2018, as buyers leaned heavily on Brazil and Argentina. Signal Ocean cargo flow analytics confirm U.S.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2025-10-24_chinas-soybean-imports-pivot-south-us-shipments-vanish-in-september-as-south-ame_img_unnamed_54a89bf5d3e3.png
China's soybean supply chain took a sharp turn south in September. No U.S.- origin soybeans were discharged at Chinese ports, the first zero month since 2018, as buyers leaned heavily on Brazil and Argentina.

children.title: U.S. cargo flows to China - collapse by September 2025
section_path: U.S. cargo flows to China - collapse by September 2025
children.section_path_text: U.S. cargo flows to China - collapse by September 2025
children.summary:
Shipments reached a peak of 4.1 Mt in January and decreased to 2.7 Mt in February, then fell to zero in September 2025; 2025 clearly differs from 2024, confirming customs reports of no U.S. Image reference: unnamed (1).png | assets/2025-10-24_chinas-soybean-imports-pivot-south-us-shipments-vanish-in-september-as-south-ame_img_unnamed28129_276a28fe257b.png
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2rk223/541973999/h/gVaVedBc2VvRQH0UdhcEwFF8nYJVtR35VNF3knbYAAw

children.title: Argentina steps up - export incentives spark surge
section_path: Argentina steps up - export incentives spark surge
children.section_path_text: Argentina steps up - export incentives spark surge
children.summary: From August to October 2025, shipments experienced a significant increase. September saw nearly 1.5 Mt, approximately double the 2024 figures, largely due to temporary export-tax relief and a weakened peso, which enhanced export competitiveness.

children.title: Brazil maintains dominance
children.summary:
Sustained high volumes from April to August (10-11 Mt/month) and elevated September shipments versus previous years highlight Brazil's extended export window and robust port capacity, though the 22.8% MoM decline in September reflects Argentina's seasonal return to the export market. Image reference: unnamed (3).png | assets/2025-10-24_chinas-soybean-imports-pivot-south-us-shipments-vanish-in-september-as-south-ame_img_unnamed28329_3ab9951198b3.png
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2rk223/541973999/h/gVaVedBc2VvRQH0UdhcEwFF8nYJVtR35VNF3knbYAAw

children.title: Market reaction and outlook
children.summary: CBOT soybean futures climbed to a one-month high amid speculation that China may resume U.S. purchases, following renewed signals in U.S.-China trade talks.

children.title: Methodology
children.summary: Data compiled from Signal Ocean Cargo Flow Analytics (Dry Bulk: Agricultural Products | Destination: China), comparing 2023-2025 monthly flows by origin (U.S., Argentina, Brazil), indicates declining U.S. shipments and stronger South American exports.
